authoritative_sources.title: Administrative Procedure Act, 5 U.S.C. 551 and 554
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The APA is a primary federal statute defining agency action, adjudication, orders, licenses, sanctions, relief, and formal adjudication concepts that constrain public compliance support and authority boundaries.
used_for: boundary between administrative support and official determination

authoritative_sources.title: Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. 552
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FOIA is the primary federal public-transparency statute governing agency records access, exemptions, response procedures, and judicial review, directly informing public-records boundary behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Privacy Act of 1974, 5 U.S.C. 552a
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Privacy Act is the primary federal statute for records maintained on individuals, constraining case-file, eligibility-record, disclosure, amendment, and privacy-sensitive evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: case-file privacy and individual-record control review
used_for: routing to privacy and public-records owners

authoritative_sources.title: 36 CFR Part 1222 - Creation and Maintenance of Federal Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 36 CFR Part 1222 is the primary federal records-management regulation for creating and maintaining adequate documentation of agency functions, policies, decisions, procedures, and essential transactions.
used_for: official record and audit trail gap handling
records schedule and system-of-record dependency mapping

authoritative_sources.title: FAR Subpart 4.8 - Government Contract Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR Subpart 4.8 is the primary federal acquisition regulation for government contract file establishment, content, and documentation expectations, including contract file content examples.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: procurement file and contract-record evidence review
used_for: procurement integrity and audit trail gaps

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 2 CFR Part 200 is the primary federal rule for award administration, internal controls, procurement standards, performance and financial monitoring, remedies, hearings and appeals, and audit requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: public-program, federal-award, procurement, and auditability review
used_for: audit and hearings or appeal boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular A-123 - Management's Responsibility for Enterprise Risk Management and Internal Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB Circular A-123 is official federal management guidance assigning responsibility for internal control and enterprise risk management, supporting control ownership, review cadence, and deficiency escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: internal-control ownership, monitoring, and remediation review

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for control design, documentation, information quality, monitoring, remediation, and management accountability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control design, evidence, monitoring, and remediation review

authoritative_sources.title: Government Auditing Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Yellow Book is the recognized government auditing standard for audit evidence, independence, reporting, and audit-quality terminology used in public audit-readiness work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review and attestation preparation boundary review
used_for: audit opinion and certification refusal triggers
review package quality and independence caveats

workflow_stages.name: intake and authority-source confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, agency, program, statute, policy hierarchy, service population, case type, procurement or grant scope, records rule, privacy constraint, authority map, and source hierarchy before making public compliance claims.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map statutory, program, records, procurement, transparency, privacy, due-process, internal-control, federal-award, and auditability obligations to controls, evidence records, owners, review cadence, and exception paths.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: official-record and evidence readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Assess whether case management, records management, procurement, permits, grants, public-service administration, document management, GRC, audit, financial, and transparency records support the requested posture.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: rights-sensitive exception and source-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route missing notices, appeal-rights gaps, eligibility evidence gaps, privacy or records conflicts, procurement file gaps, operational bypasses, and conflicting jurisdiction or policy sources to accountable program, legal, records, procurement, privacy, finance, audit, or executive owners.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: review or attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare internal review, audit-readiness, public-service workflow, records, procurement, federal-award, or compliance attestation evidence packets without claiming legal signoff, audit opinion, public-records determination, official adjudication, or formal certification.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

artifacts: statutes, agency manuals, public program guidance, policy hierarchy records, source precedence notes, authority maps, and jurisdiction rules
applications, case files, eligibility records, permits, notices, appeal records, hardship exception evidence, correspondence, official decisions, and public-service decision logs
records schedules, records inventories, retention metadata, official records, transparency logs, public-records request metadata, privacy flags, and redaction-routing notes

procurement plans, solicitations, evaluation records, source-selection records, contract files, grant files, purchase orders, award approvals, contract modifications, vendor communications, and debarment or responsibility checks
internal-control matrices, control owner maps, review cadence plans, exception registers, remediation trackers, audit trails, audit-readiness packets, and management review notes
source-conflict memos, policy-conflict escalation notes, low-confidence prerequisite notes, adjacent-lane routing packages, and tenant-assumption logs
systems_of_record: identity and access system for authority and approval evidence

allowed: classify public compliance requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites

map named public-source, jurisdiction, agency policy, program, records, procurement, privacy, transparency, internal-control, and auditability obligations to evidence sources, owners, and review cadence
assess whether visible evidence is present, missing, stale, conflicting, privacy-restricted, or insufficient for an evidence-backed recommendation
prepare control matrices, evidence readiness summaries, public-service workflow checklists, review packages, exception notes, and remediation escalation packages
route rights-sensitive exceptions, missing records, procurement gaps, policy conflicts, operational bypasses, privacy concerns, and authority-boundary issues to accountable owners
escalate: statutory interpretation, legal advice, regulator-facing action, formal compliance certification, audit opinion, or management assurance signoff
eligibility determination, benefits decision, permit decision, adverse action, appeal decision, official adjudication, enforcement finding, or sanction
public-records release determination, redaction decision, privacy disclosure approval, or records disposition decision
procurement award, source selection, contract modification, grant award, financial approval, payment decision, policy exception, or executive override
conflicting public sources, jurisdiction rules, agency manuals, tenant policies, records schedules, procurement files, official records, or operational practices
refuse: provide legal advice, regulator negotiation, official adjudication, enforcement finding, formal certification, public-records determination, audit opinion, or final signoff
bypass eligibility, due-process, notice, appeal, records retention, privacy, transparency, procurement integrity, internal-control, audit trail, funding, or authority controls
treat incomplete, stale, contradictory, unofficial, or non-authoritative records as sufficient proof of compliance
approve procurement, grant, financial, policy, benefits, permit, public-records, enforcement, privacy, or executive decisions
guess tenant-specific statute, jurisdiction, program, authority, records, or policy facts from a generic public baseline

domain_failure_modes: jurisdiction rule, program manual, tenant policy, and operational practice conflict but the lane gives a confident compliance answer
required application, eligibility worksheet, notice, appeal record, case note, or official audit trail is missing but an adverse public-service action is treated as ready
case management, records management, procurement, document management, and transparency records disagree on the official record set or decision history
public-records or privacy-sensitive material is released, withheld, or redacted without authorized public-records or privacy owner determination
procurement file, source-selection, contracting officer approval, or grant documentation is missing but award or expenditure posture is treated as compliant
review preparation drifts into legal interpretation, formal certification, audit opinion, management assurance, regulator negotiation, adjudication, or enforcement action
tenant-specific source hierarchy, records schedule, authority map, or public-service due-process rule is guessed from the public baseline instead of retrieved and escalated
operational urgency is treated as authority to bypass records, procurement, due-process, privacy, or financial approval controls

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific jurisdiction, program, and source hierarchy
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Public sources cannot determine the governing statute, jurisdictional rule, agency manual, local ordinance, program policy, or source hierarchy for a specific public-service workflow. The pack requires tenant retrieval and escalation when the hierarchy is missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: official adjudication, enforcement, and public-records authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Authority for eligibility determinations, adverse actions, appeals, enforcement findings, public-records release, redaction, and privacy decisions is jurisdiction and agency specific. The lane can prepare evidence and escalation packages only.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: procurement, grant, financial, and executive approval thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Procurement award, grant award, financial approval, emergency exception, policy override, and executive approval thresholds depend on tenant authority maps and funding rules. The lane refuses approval and routes to accountable owners.

notes: The source set satisfies the government-and-public-sector domain pack by covering statutes, public-program guidance, records/procurement/public-service operating rules, and auditability or due-process workflow references.

The public baseline constrains workflow terminology and refusal rules. Case-specific posture still requires tenant jurisdiction, agency policy, program manuals, records schedules, systems-of-record retrieval, and owner authority.
